title: Add real install.ps1 -> Scheduled Task -> crash-restart -> uninstall smoke test to release-windows
action:
Add scripts/windows-install-smoke.ps1 and wire it into .github/workflows/release.yml's release-windows job.

1. New step in release-windows, appended immediately AFTER the existing 'Upload to release' step (per D1/D10) -- do not create a new job. Set env HERDR_GO_VERSION: ${{ github.ref_name }} (per D2, pins the exact tag under test, never 'latest') and invoke: pwsh -NoProfile -File scripts/windows-install-smoke.ps1.

2. scripts/windows-install-smoke.ps1 runs the LOCAL checked-out install.ps1 (& (Join-Path $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE 'install.ps1'), not irm from raw.githubusercontent -- deterministic against the exact commit under test) with $env:HERDR_GO_VERSION already set by the workflow step. Assert $env:HERDR_GO_VERSION is set and not 'latest' before proceeding.

3. After install.ps1 completes (it already calls Start-ScheduledTask itself), assert: Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName 'HerdrGo' exists; poll http://127.0.0.1:8787/api/health until it returns HTTP 200 (per D8 -- any 200 response is sufficient, herdr_up need not be true, do not wire a Herdr binary into the task's environment).

4. Capture the login token by reading the token file under $env:APPDATA/herdr-go/herdr-go.env (same read pattern as windows-runtime-smoke.ps1's Read-SmokeTail/token parsing). The moment the token value is known, emit Write-Host "::add-mask::$token" (per D9) before it can appear in any further output; reuse or adapt windows-runtime-smoke.ps1's Redact-SmokeLog pattern for any diagnostic log tails.

5. Crash simulation (per D11): find the running process via Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.Path -eq (Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'herdr-go\bin\herdr-go.exe') }, then taskkill.exe /PID <id> /T /F. Confirm /api/health stops responding (or the process is gone), then wait up to the RestartInterval + buffer (~90s, per D5 -- a real wait, never simulated/skipped) for /api/health to return 200 again, proving the Scheduled Task's real recovery.

6. Run install.ps1 -Uninstall (same local checked-out script). Assert: Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName 'HerdrGo' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue is null; the installed binary under %LOCALAPPDATA%\herdr-go\bin is gone; config.json, the token file, and any data under %APPDATA%\herdr-go are still present and unchanged (per D6, reusing cross-platform-install CONTEXT.md's D10 contract).

7. Wrap the sequence in try/finally: on any assertion failure, still attempt to remove the Scheduled Task and kill any leftover herdr-go.exe process before exiting, so the runner is left clean regardless of pass/fail (matches windows-runtime-smoke.ps1's existing finally-block discipline).

Do not touch scripts/windows-runtime-smoke.ps1 (per D3) -- it keeps proving the binary-level Herdr round-trip independently.

truths: The new step in release-windows runs only after the existing 'Upload to release' step, using HERDR_GO_VERSION pinned to github.ref_name -- never 'latest' (D1/D2/D10).

The script runs the local checked-out install.ps1, registers/starts the real Scheduled Task 'HerdrGo', and proves liveness via /api/health returning 200 without wiring any Herdr binary into the task's environment (D8).

The script kills the running herdr-go.exe process (matched by installed BinPath) and proves the Scheduled Task's RestartInterval-based recovery via a real wait for /api/health to respond again (D5/D11).
install.ps1 -Uninstall is run for real afterward; the Scheduled Task and installed binary are gone, config/data/token files under %APPDATA%\herdr-go are left untouched (D6).
The captured login token is masked via ::add-mask:: immediately once known; no plaintext token appears un-masked in any script output (D9).
A try/finally ensures the Scheduled Task and any running herdr-go.exe process are cleaned up even when an assertion fails midway.

artifacts.substantive: real PowerShell implementing install -> verify-live -> crash -> verify-restart -> uninstall -> verify-clean, not a stub or placeholder
a real new step appended to the release-windows job after 'Upload to release', invoking the new script with HERDR_GO_VERSION pinned to github.ref_name

key_links: release.yml's new step sets HERDR_GO_VERSION so install.ps1 (which already reads $env:HERDR_GO_VERSION) fetches the exact tag under test
the script's liveness/crash-recovery checks call http://127.0.0.1:8787/api/health, matching src/web/api.rs:66-74 (200 regardless of herdr_up, no auth required)
prohibitions: Do not add draft-release/promote logic (D1 rejected pre-publish gating).
Do not wire a Herdr binary into the Scheduled Task's environment (D8).
Do not use HERDR_GO_VERSION=latest anywhere in the new step (D2).

verify: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))" && test -f scripts/windows-install-smoke.ps1 && cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash tests/rename_contract.sh && cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: Added scripts/windows-install-smoke.ps1 (install.ps1 -> Scheduled Task -> crash-restart -> uninstall lifecycle smoke, with token echo suppressed/captured-and-masked per D9) and wired it into release-windows in .github/workflows/release.yml as a post-publish step (HERDR_GO_VERSION pinned to github.ref_name, per D1/D2). Local verify green; real correctness proven on next real release CI run (D12).

Per locked decision D12, real correctness of the new PowerShell/Scheduled-Task logic (install/crash-restart/uninstall) is proven only by an actual windows-2022 CI run on the next real release tag, not locally -- this dev box has no pwsh installed.
